title: Bumble Full Year 2024 Earnings: EPS Misses Expectations
text: Bumble reported full year 2024 earnings with revenue of US$1.07b, up 1.9% from FY 2023. The company posted a net loss of US$557.0 million, which represented a substantial deterioration compared to the prior year. Most notably, earnings per share declined to a loss of US$4.61, a dramatic worsening from the US$0.032 loss recorded in 2023. The company's revenue matched analyst expectations, but the earnings metric underperformed. Earnings per share (EPS) missed analyst estimates by 4.2%, indicating the bottom line fell short of market expectations. Looking ahead, the company faces headwinds with revenue projected to decline by 1.2% annually over the next three years, while competitors in the interactive media industry are expected to grow at approximately 10% annually. This suggests Bumble is losing ground relative to its sector. Investor sentiment turned negative, with the company's shares down 12% from a week ago, reflecting disappointment in the financial results and forward guidance. Despite challenges, the stock is characterized as very undervalued with moderate growth potential, suggesting the market may be pricing in excessive pessimism.
